economic_notes: Nation operates a retail segment (102009262 Saskatchewan Ltd.), bison operations (Kinistin Bison Inc.), and economic development segment. Nation is pursuing agricultural implements claim and timber claim against the Crown. Multiple capital projects underway including Water Treatment Plant ($6.15M budget), School Upgrade ($5.94M budget), and CMHC Rapid Housing.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022. Two wholly owned subsidiaries consolidated: Kinistin Bison Inc. and 102009262 Saskatchewan Ltd. (retail). Cost-method investments in STC Investments Limited Partnership (14%) and STC Casino Holdings Corporation (14%) not consolidated. Nation has agricultural implements claim loan ($938,308) and timber claim loan ($61,021) from Government of Canada, both non-interest bearing. Nation not in compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act due to late audit report (dated December 9, 2022, past July 29, 2022 deadline). Approximately $980,000 in accounts receivable from STC Health and Family Services Inc. accrued over several years with collectability uncertainty. Operating deficit of $587,033 included in accumulated surplus. Total government transfers calculated from deferred revenue note contributions recognized: ISC $7,339,852 + CMHC $1,444,655 + STC Health $3,190,498 + FNIHB $874,533 + STC $533,795 = $13,383,333. OSR = $14,198,020 - $13,383,333 = $814,687.
